question: What did Winston Churchill vow about the beaches in his June 1940 speech to the House of Commons?
reference_answer: Churchill vowed, "we will fight them on the beaches and we will never surrender" [S1].

must_support.claim: In the June 1940 speech Churchill declared, "we will fight them on the beaches and we will never surrender."
must_support.supporting_quote: Addressing the Commons on 4 June 1940, Churchill resolved: "we shall fight on the beaches, we shall fight on the landing grounds, we shall fight in the fields and in the streets, we shall fight in the hills; we shall never surrender."

must_not_assert: Churchill said "we will fight them on the beaches and we will never surrender."
